title: Black Book single-project production isolation matrix

migrationTriggers: Production data or credentials must be used in a non-production environment.
Research workload risk, cost, or compliance can no longer be contained by per-service IAM, bucket IAM, database roles, quotas, and kill switches.
Independent environment recovery, billing, residency, or organization-policy boundaries become mandatory.

projects.role: blackbook-prod: public serving (App Hosting, Firestore public/** projections + create-only submissions, Auth, App Check enforced, prod secrets only)
pre-production mirror of blackbook-prod, synthetic data only, minInstances 0
research pipeline + admin console; named Firestore databases raw-ingest and curated with per-database IAM conditions; admin runs Cloud Run + IAP attached directly to the service (no load balancer)

notes: Machine-readable source of truth for BB-005 isolation inside the existing production project black-book-efaaf.
The project and Hosting site are live; Firebase apps, App Hosting backends, service accounts, buckets, database roles, and listed IAM bindings are designed but not yet verified as provisioned.
Development remains local and emulator-only. Staging is a configuration namespace or separately named App Hosting backend in the same production project, not a security boundary.
Human-readable companions: projects.matrix.md, service-accounts.matrix.md, storage-buckets.matrix.md, iam-boundaries.md, wif/.
BB-010 WIF/OIDC deploy identities are designed under infra/gcp/wif/ (not applied; GitHub numeric IDs TBD until remote exists).
No secrets are stored here; secret material lives only in Secret Manager per environment.

BB-078 (ADR-012, 2026-07-17): mode/currentProject/deferredMultiProjectTarget above still describe the LIVE single-project state and are unchanged by this update. productionResplitTarget and the crossProjectGrants entries below describe the ADR-012 three-project target, not yet applied (BB-079). serviceAccounts entries promotion and submissions-puller are ADR-012-target-only identities living in blackbook-internal, not black-book-efaaf.

black-book-2ve (BB-078 course-correction, 2026-07-17): serviceAccounts entries admin, research, publication, and security, and the private-evidence bucket entry, are updated in place to their ADR-012 target project (blackbook-internal) - they were left describing the superseded black-book-efaaf placement after BB-078, which was itself the gap this fix closes (see docs/runbooks/production-cloud-apply-checklist.md's 'Gaps found during consolidation' #1-#2). infra/gcp/terraform/locals.tf and buckets.tf now match: those four identities and the private-evidence bucket are no longer created in blackbook-prod's Terraform. The environments[] array's production-research entry is intentionally left describing the pre-ADR-012 same-project namespace concept as historical record, same convention as deferredMultiProjectTarget.

environments.purpose: Local development through demo-black-book emulators and synthetic fixtures; no deployed development cloud environment.
environments.humanAccess: developers use local emulators; any access to black-book-efaaf is production access
environments.notes: AC-ISO-1 project separation is not currently available. Tests and local development must fail closed against black-book-efaaf.

environments.purpose: Optional pre-production configuration namespace or separately named App Hosting backend; it shares the production trust boundary.
environments.configurationScope: proposed App Hosting backend black-book-web-staging and staging-prefixed resource names
environments.humanAccess: CI (staging deploy identity via WIF) + break-glass
environments.notes: Not provisioned and not a security boundary. Must use synthetic data and separate names; never treat it as safe for destructive tests.

environments.purpose: The one live Firebase/GCP production project for public serving, canonical data, publication, research, and security workloads.
environments.configurationScope: production resources and proposed App Hosting backend black-book-web-production
environments.humanAccess: CI (production deploy identity via WIF) + protected environment approval + break-glass
environments.notes: Project number 332234323945; existing Hosting site black-book-efaaf.web.app. Treat every cloud resource here as production.

environments.purpose: Production research configuration scope inside the same project, isolated by service account, bucket IAM, database role, network policy, quota, and kill switch.
environments.configurationScope: research-prefixed jobs, secrets, logs, quotas, and private-evidence bucket
environments.humanAccess: CI (research deploy identity via WIF) + break-glass
environments.notes: Designed, not verified as provisioned. Research must never receive public-media write, publication role, release-pointer access, or public ingress.

serviceAccounts.purpose: Firebase App Hosting backend runtime identity for the public web app.
mustNotHave: any read on private-evidence, quarantine, or exports buckets
any canonical database write role
any publication or promotion permission
read on private-evidence or quarantine buckets
any write on the public schema or canonical tables
any publication, promotion, or migration permission

serviceAccounts.purpose: Corrections / contribution intake API runtime identity.
mustNotHave: read of quarantine object contents (write/create only; scanning is the security worker)
any read on private-evidence or public-media buckets
any canonical write, publication, or promotion permission
any research, publication, or deploy identity impersonation

serviceAccounts.purpose: Private publication / promotion / internal control API. Required by ADR-005 (not in the BB-005 nine but part of the same boundary).
mustNotHave: any public internet ingress
write access to raw evidence objects
callable by end-user tokens

serviceAccounts.purpose: [ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079)] Relocated from blackbook-prod (black-book-efaaf) to blackbook-internal; account id becomes admin-app there (the 5-char id 'admin' is invalid, see infra/gcp/wif/deploy-roles.md). Admin / research console runtime identity (Cloud Run + IAP, attached directly to the service - no load balancer).
roles: roles/datastore.viewer (scoped, both blackbook-internal named databases; cross-project condition, see multi-project/iam-cross-project.tf admin_reads_internal_databases)
mustNotHave: public internet ingress without IAP
ability to bypass the publication workflow for release activation
any IAM grant in blackbook-prod - ADR-012's cross-project grant list (promotion, security, submissions-puller) does not include admin-app

serviceAccounts.purpose: Schema migration (DDL) runner; short-lived, invoked only by CI deploy pipeline.
mustNotHave: runtime attachment to any long-running service
publication or promotion permission
read on private-evidence, quarantine, or public-media buckets

serviceAccounts.purpose: [ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079)] Relocated from blackbook-prod (black-book-efaaf) to blackbook-internal. Source discovery, ingestion, and evidence staging; writes only the raw-ingest named Firestore database (see multi-project/iam-cross-project.tf research_writes_raw_ingest) - never curated, never blackbook-prod.
roles: roles/datastore.user (scoped to raw-ingest named database only, blackbook-internal)
mustNotHave: any publication, deployment, project-admin, or service-account impersonation role
write on public-media or public projections
publication, promotion, or release-activation permission
ability to modify canonical release pointers
any IAM grant in blackbook-prod or blackbook-staging
write access to the curated named database

serviceAccounts.purpose: [ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079)] Relocated from blackbook-prod (black-book-efaaf) to blackbook-internal, and its role narrows to the curation worker: reads raw-ingest, writes curated (see multi-project/iam-cross-project.tf publication_reads_raw_ingest / publication_writes_curated). The prior projection/snapshot/release-worker role - including the public-media/exports bucket writes below - now belongs to the new cross-project promotion identity (see serviceAccounts[].id=promotion), not this one.
roles: roles/datastore.viewer (scoped to raw-ingest named database, blackbook-internal)
roles/datastore.user (scoped to curated named database, blackbook-internal)
mustNotHave: write access to raw evidence objects
ability to alter raw evidence or research staging
public internet ingress
any IAM grant in blackbook-prod - cross-project public/** writes belong only to promotion

serviceAccounts.purpose: [ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079)] Relocated from blackbook-prod (black-book-efaaf) to blackbook-internal. Quarantine, content validation, malware scan, integrity worker. Required by ADR-005 (not in the BB-005 nine).

bucketAccess.access: objectAdmin (cross-project into blackbook-prod, see crossProjectGrants id=security-quarantine-prod)
objectCreator (cross-project into blackbook-prod, post-scan promotion, see crossProjectGrants id=security-quarantine-prod)

mustNotHave: public internet ingress
publication or release-activation permission

serviceAccounts.purpose: Database backup, point-in-time-recovery, and export runner.
mustNotHave: any write on canonical tables or public projections
publication or promotion permission
bucket access unless a separately reviewed backup export bucket is added

serviceAccounts.purpose: Production deployment identity impersonated by GitHub Actions via Workload Identity Federation (ADR-006 / BB-010). No standing JSON keys.
mustNotHave: trust from unprotected branches, pull requests, forks, or unapproved GitHub environments
runtime attachment to an application or worker
long-lived exported service-account keys

serviceAccounts.purpose: ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079): the only identity anywhere, including any blackbook-prod-native identity, that writes blackbook-prod's public/** Firestore projections. Reads the curated named database in blackbook-internal; writes cross-project into blackbook-prod.
roles: roles/datastore.user (scoped to blackbook-prod public/** via IAM condition, cross-project)
mustNotHave: write access to raw-ingest (reads curated only)
any IAM grant in blackbook-staging
public internet ingress
ability to be invoked by an end-user token

serviceAccounts.surface: workers/submissions-puller (new logical surface, ADR-012; implementation out of scope for BB-078)
serviceAccounts.purpose: ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079): reverses the submissions data-flow direction. Pulls new documents from blackbook-prod's create-only submissions collection into blackbook-internal for triage; blackbook-prod never pushes into blackbook-internal.
roles: roles/datastore.viewer (scoped to blackbook-prod submissions collection via IAM condition, cross-project, read-only)
mustNotHave: write access to any blackbook-prod resource
read access to blackbook-prod public/** projections beyond the submissions collection
any IAM grant in blackbook-staging

buckets.publicAccessPrevention: inherited (the single documented public-serving exception; prefer CDN-fronted delivery)
buckets.notes: Only vetted, scanned, promoted media lands here. Written only after security validation.

[ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079)] Relocated from blackbook-prod (black-book-efaaf) to blackbook-internal because both writers (research, security) move there too - colocating the bucket with its only writers is more defensible than keeping it in blackbook-prod and adding a new cross-project grant. See infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/buckets.tf. Raw research evidence. Never public. api-internal (blackbook-prod) is no longer a reader: the ADR-012 'prod -> internal: none' invariant means no blackbook-prod principal may hold any IAM in blackbook-internal, and moving the bucket removes the need for a cross-project grant that would otherwise be required to preserve that read.
Generated exports delivered via short-TTL signed URLs; the bucket itself is never public.
Untrusted intake. Public Access Prevention enforced so quarantine objects can never be served publicly. Only the security worker reads/scans.

crossProjectGrants.rationale: ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079): promotion is the only identity anywhere - including any blackbook-prod-native identity - that writes public projections. One-way push from blackbook-internal; no prod principal has any reciprocal grant into blackbook-internal.

ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079): mirrors the prior same-project publication bucket grants (see serviceAccounts[].id=publication), now cross-project because public-media/exports live in blackbook-prod and the promotion pipeline runs in blackbook-internal.

crossProjectGrants.to: blackbook-prod quarantine bucket (admin) and public-media bucket (create)
crossProjectGrants.rationale: ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079): same logical worker as serviceAccounts[].id=security above, which moves from black-book-efaaf to blackbook-internal at BB-079 migration time. Quarantine stays co-located with the public intake surface (api-submissions) in blackbook-prod; security scans and promotes cross-project from blackbook-internal.

ADR-012 target, not yet applied (BB-079): reverses the pre-ADR-012 submissions flow. blackbook-internal pulls new submissions from prod's create-only collection for triage instead of prod pushing into a shared research-adjacent store. No corresponding write grant exists in either direction for this identity.

orgPolicies.setting: enforced on black-book-efaaf where possible; public-media requires a documented exception or private CDN origin design
restrict IAM members to the org domain (block external principals)
enforced (deploy uses WIF, not exported keys)
restrict resource creation to approved US locations

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Development credentials cannot access production (not currently enforceable as a cloud project boundary)
enforcedBy: N/A as an environment-to-project invariant: black-book-efaaf is the only cloud project and is production.
Local development and automated tests use demo-black-book emulators or disposable local services and fail closed when production identifiers are present.
Any human or CI access to black-book-efaaf is production access; github-deploy is WIF-only and requires protected production context.
A separate cloud development project must be created before cloud-based development credentials or destructive integration tests are allowed.

[ADR-012 target, not yet applied] blackbook-staging gives cloud-based pre-production testing a real project boundary from blackbook-prod, satisfying the prior N/A trigger. github-deploy stays project-scoped to blackbook-prod with no binding in blackbook-staging or blackbook-internal.
research@black-book-efaaf is a distinct identity with no publication, deployment, service-account impersonation, release-pointer, or public-media write permission.
role_research can write evidence staging and read a canonical subset but cannot write public projections or activate releases.
Publication requires publication/api-internal identities even though they share the project (ADR-005, ADR-009).
research SA has no bucketAccess to public-media and no release-pointer permission.

[ADR-012 target, not yet applied] research@blackbook-internal has no IAM binding in blackbook-prod at all (project boundary, not just role absence). Only promotion@blackbook-internal has a prod write grant (see crossProjectGrants), and promotion has no write access to the raw-ingest named database - only read on curated - so a compromised research adapter cannot reach promotion's prod grant via a shared database.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Public services cannot read private evidence blobs
enforcedBy: web-runtime and api-public have no IAM binding on the private-evidence bucket.
private-evidence has Public Access Prevention enforced and UBLA enabled; only research/security write and only publication/admin/api-internal read.
Project-level storage roles are forbidden for runtime identities because they would bypass per-bucket separation.

[ADR-012 target, not yet applied] private-evidence moves to blackbook-internal, a project web-runtime/api-public (in blackbook-prod) have no membership in - project boundary plus bucket IAM, not bucket IAM alone.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Quarantine objects cannot be served publicly
enforcedBy: quarantine bucket has constraints/storage.publicAccessPrevention enforced and UBLA enabled (no allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers possible).
Only api-submissions (create) and security (read/scan) touch it; web-runtime and api-public have no access.
No CDN backend or signed-URL issuer is wired to the quarantine bucket.

[ADR-012 target, not yet applied] quarantine stays in blackbook-prod, co-located with the api-submissions surface that creates it; the only cross-project reader is security@blackbook-internal (see crossProjectGrants id=security-quarantine-prod), which holds no publish or release-activation grant in any project.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Compromise of submissions service does not grant canonical or publication permissions
enforcedBy: api-submissions SA holds only role_submissions_write (write to quarantine/staging tables), objectCreator on quarantine, and scoped secret access.
It has no canonical write, no publication/promotion permission, no private-evidence read, and no identity-impersonation access.
Promotion to public requires the separate publication workflow via internal/publication identities (ADR-004, ADR-005).

[ADR-012 target, not yet applied] even a full blackbook-prod project compromise (not just api-submissions) cannot reach blackbook-internal to fabricate a promotion, because promotion is a pull initiated from blackbook-internal (submissions-puller@blackbook-internal reads prod's create-only collection; see crossProjectGrants) - there is no callable path from prod into internal in either direction.
